title: Consolidate direct flexible-child helpers into `MLF.Binding.Children`
problem: `src/MLF/Binding/Tree.hs` and `src/MLF/Binding/Validation.hs` each define the same three-step helper cluster: `tyVarChildFilter`, a `collectBoundChildren` wrapper specialized to `BindFlex`, and `boundFlexChildren`. This duplicates one structural query over the binding tree, increases drift risk, and already partially overlaps with `src/MLF/Binding/Children.hs`, which owns the underlying generic traversal.
why_this_is_simpler: This is a small, one-round simplification because the generic traversal already lives in `MLF.Binding.Children`; the round only needs to move the TyVar-specific filter and `BindFlex` specialization to that same module, then have `Tree` and `Validation` call the shared helper. No caller behavior, paper-facing algorithm, or quotient logic needs to change.
why_thesis_exactness_should_be_preserved: The duplicated logic is not thesis policy; it is a reusable structural implementation of the paper’s direct flexibly bound child query `Q(n)` restricted to `TyVar` nodes. Both copies already implement the same predicate and same error path, and neither encodes a distinct theorem-side interpretation. Centralizing it preserves thesis exactness by keeping one canonical implementation of the existing query rather than maintaining two potentially diverging copies.
expected_benefits: Removes duplicate `TyVar`/`BindFlex` query logic from two modules
Makes `MLF.Binding.Children` the single owner of binding-child enumeration helpers
Reduces risk that validation and operational tree code drift on binder-child semantics
Keeps future thesis-sensitive changes localized to one structural helper
risks: Creating a new shared export could slightly blur module boundaries if naming is too broad
A refactor could accidentally change error-context strings if not kept byte-for-byte stable
If any hidden caller depends on local helper names for tests or debug output, small adjustments may be required
required_validation: Run targeted tests covering binding-tree validation and binder enumeration behavior
Add or update one regression asserting `boundFlexChildren` behavior remains identical for missing binders and mixed child kinds
Run `cabal build all && cabal test` before closing the round
